title: Thickly Tava-Cured Norwegian Salmon Patties
description: Savory, crispy, hangover-cure Norwegian dish featuring golden syrup and shio-koji-cure, vegan-friendly and perfect for a late morning meal.

ingredients.name: Norwegian salmon fillet
ingredients.notes: Choose a firm, oily fish for a flaky patty

ingredients.name: Shio-koji cure
ingredients.notes: A traditional Japanese seasoning made from rice koji, salt, and other ingredients

ingredients.name: Golden syrup
ingredients.notes: A thick, amber liquid made from refined sugar cane

ingredients.name: Lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Add a squeeze of fresh lemon for brightness and balance

ingredients.name: Rice flour
ingredients.notes: For a gluten-free and crispy coating

ingredients.name: Tava oil
ingredients.notes: A neutral-tasting oil for high-heat cooking

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Enhance the natural flavor of the salmon

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Add a touch of spice for depth

ingredients.name: Chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: A fresh, herbaceous flavor for garnish

ingredients.name: Chopped fresh dill
ingredients.notes: A classic Scandinavian herb for added freshness

instructions: Prepare a shio-koji cure by mixing the cure with 2 tbsp of water to create a smooth paste.
Mix the salmon fillet, cure, golden syrup, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper in a bowl until just combined.
Cover the mixture with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a tava or griddle over medium-high heat to 400F (200C).
Divide the mixture into 6 portions and shape each portion into a patty.
Coat each patty with rice flour and tap off any excess.
Add the patties to the preheated tava or griddle and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until golden and crispy.
Transfer the patties to a plate and garnish with chopped parsley and dill.
